body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

Claude Code has a family of synchronous, local-only slash commands that never round-trip to the model: `/help`, `/cost`, `/status`, `/model` (the picker), `/context`, `/clear` (UI-side), `/config`, etc. When the user invokes one of these, the TUI renders the response locally; if anything lands in the JSONL transcript at all, it's a local-command wrapper, not a real user turn or assistant message.

The parser explicitly recognises and discards these wrappers. `isLocalCommandContent` (parser.go:216-225) returns true for content starting with `<local-command`, `<command-name>`, `<bash-input>`, or `<bash-stdout>`, and callers drop the event before it reaches state machinery. That's exactly the right behaviour for this scenario.

The spec's two Expected bullets:

**Bullet 1 — state stays `ready` throughout.** Because no genuine `user` message is appended (only the local-command wrapper, which the parser filters), the daemon emits no state_transition for the slash command. The session sits in `ready` before, during, and after. The phase contract for this scenario asserts a `ready` invariant across the slash-command phase.

**Bullet 2 — no `→ working` transition recorded for the slash command itself.** Direct consequence of the above. The parser's local-command filter is the load-bearing piece here, and `parser_test.go` covers it as a regression test.

## What irrlicht does NOT see (and why that's OK)

The slash command's textual response (e.g. `/cost` output, `/help` text) is rendered in the TUI but never appears as a transcript event, so the daemon has nothing to surface. This is correct: the scenario's premise is precisely that there's no model call to observe.

## Caveat on which commands count

Not every slash command is synchronous. `/compact` triggers a real model call (context summarisation) and belongs to `Context compaction`. `/init` writes files via tool calls. The recipe must restrict itself to genuinely local commands (`/help`, `/cost`, `/status`, `/model`) to satisfy the spec's no-LLM-call premise.
caveats: `/clear` resets the conversation and mints a fresh session UUID — observable as a `session_birth` event. That belongs to the `Session reset` scenario, not this one. The recipe must avoid it.
`/compact` and `/init` call the model and would invalidate this scenario. Stick to `/help`, `/cost`, `/status`, `/model`.

If Anthropic changes the TUI to log slash-command invocations as real user messages, this scenario would start failing. The parser's local-command filter is the contract that catches such regressions.

sources.note: Section 'Feature: Synchronous slash command (no LLM call)' (lines 140-145) — two Expected bullets.
isLocalCommandContent (216-225) filters <local-command>, <command-name>, <bash-input>, <bash-stdout> wrappers so they don't drive state.
Parser test suite covers local-command filtering — regression guard for this scenario.
